SHERIFF'S CLAIM OF EXEMPTION PACKET (CONFIDENTIAL)

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT TANIA SHEPPARD'S CLAIM OF EXEMPTION IS DENIED. ALTHOUGH MS. SHEPPARD HAS 2 DEPENDENT CHILDREN WHO NEED SCHOOL AND CHILD CARE, SHE EXPRESSED NO OBJECTION TO THE COURT'S TENTATIVE RULING. THEREFORE, THE COURT ORDERED THAT $150 SHALL BE WITHHELD FROM DEFENDANT'S INCOME PER PAY PERIOD TO SATISFY THE DEBT OWED. THE LEVYING OFFICER IS ALSO DIRECTED TO RELEASE ANY FUNDS HELD TO THE JUDGMENT DEBTOR. (ORDER SIGNED AFTER HEARING.) JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

calendar.matters:
Ntc Of Hrg On Claim Of Exemption; Notice Of Opposition
Off Calendar Sep-16-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Tania Sheppard, Tel. 480.390.1342, defendant/judgment debtor/claimant appearing on her own behalf;
No other appearance.

RULING: The matter was discussed. After hearing, the Court adopted the tentative ruling. DEFENDANT TANIA SHEPPARD's Claim Of Exemption is denied. Although Ms. Sheppard has 2 dependent children who need school and child care, she expressed no objection to the court's tentative ruling. Therefore, the Court ordered that $150 shall be withheld from defendant's income per pay period to satisfy the debt owed. The levying officer is also directed to release any funds held to the judgment debtor. (Order signed after hearing.) Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185
